name: What’s realistic timing for a permit‑ready kitchen remodel in San Bruno or Daly City?

mainEntity.name: From first design meeting to final inspection, how long does a typical permitted kitchen remodel take in San Bruno or Daly City, and what usually causes delays?
mainEntity.text: From first design meeting to final inspection, how long does a typical permitted kitchen remodel take in San Bruno or Daly City, and what usually causes delays?

acceptedAnswer.text: A realistic timeline for a full, permitted kitchen remodel in San Bruno or Daly City has two main phases: pre‑construction and on‑site work. Design, selections, and permitting can take 4–8 weeks, depending on how quickly you make decisions and the city’s review queue. During this time, your contractor should finalize layout, electrical and plumbing changes, and submit permit documents while ordering long‑lead items like cabinets and some appliances. Once demolition begins, straightforward projects typically take 8–16 weeks of on‑site work. The sequence usually goes: demo, rough plumbing and electrical, inspections, insulation and drywall, flooring, cabinets, countertops, tile, trim, painting, fixture and appliance install, then final inspections. Common delays include permit review taking longer than expected, custom cabinets arriving late, or appliances being back‑ordered. Changes after work starts—such as moving a wall or changing tile—can require re‑planning and sometimes revised inspections. Failed inspections for items like GFCI placement or range hood venting can also push things back. Ask your contractor for a written schedule that highlights critical path items and contingency time. The more you finalize layout, materials, and appliance specs before permits are submitted, the more likely your project stays close to the original timeline.
acceptedAnswer.description: Permit‑ready kitchen remodels in San Bruno or Daly City commonly run 8–16 weeks of construction after 4–8 weeks of design, permitting, and ordering. Delays often stem from permit review, custom cabinet or appliance lead times, change orders, and failed or rescheduled inspections. Agreeing on all selections early reduces most timing risks.
